issue_title: Uncaught NPE in siteResponseToSiteModel when WP.com returns blogging_prompts_settings with null reminders_days/reminders_time

reasoning_trace.finding: Search located the reported mapping method and all suspicious dereferences in libs/fluxc/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/fluxc/network/rest/wpcom/site/SiteRestClient.kt, plus the Java response fields in SiteWPComRestResponse.java.
Repository instructions emphasize avoiding unsafe null assertions and treating Java platform types carefully; no code changes were required for this task.
Confirmed project structure: FluxC under libs/fluxc is the networking/data layer where WP.com site responses are parsed.
fetchSites() iterates WP.com site responses and calls siteResponseToSiteModel(siteResponse); fetchSite() calls siteResponseToSiteModel(response.data) on successful REST responses.
fetchWPComSiteByUrl() also maps a SiteWPComRestResponse through siteResponseToSiteModel(response) in its success callback.
siteResponseToSiteModel() guards from.options and the parent blogging_prompts_settings object, but then dereferences it.reminders_days and it.reminders_time without null-safe access.
Options.blogging_prompts_settings, BloggingPromptsSettings.reminders_days, and BloggingPromptsSettings.reminders_time are public Java fields with no non-null annotation or default initialization.

reasoning_trace.target: A non-null blogging_prompts_settings object with null reminders_days crashes on the first day lookup.
reasoning_trace.finding: In Kotlin, it.reminders_days["monday"] invokes get on the platform-typed Map receiver; if Gson left reminders_days null, the Elvis operator only applies after the receiver dereference and cannot prevent the NPE.

reasoning_trace.target: A non-null blogging_prompts_settings object with null reminders_time crashes in the time parsing block.
reasoning_trace.finding: it.reminders_time.split(".") dereferences a platform-typed String; the surrounding catch handles NumberFormatException only, so a NullPointerException escapes.
SiteRestClient tests exist, but there is no test hit for blogging_prompts_settings, reminders_days, reminders_time, or direct coverage of the private mapper path.
Tests cover fetchSite() and fetchSites() happy/error paths using minimal SiteWPComRestResponse objects, but do not construct Options.blogging_prompts_settings or null nested reminder fields.
Blogging reminder setters are simple assignments; they do not introduce the NPE and cannot catch a null receiver dereference that happens before the setter call.
SITE_FIELDS includes options, so the WP.com site fetches request the options object that contains blogging_prompts_settings.
syncGetRequest() and buildGetRequest() create WPComGsonRequest with the SiteWPComRestResponse class and no custom Gson builder for these site calls.
WPComGsonRequest delegates parsing to GsonRequest and does not add a custom deserializer for SiteWPComRestResponse.
GsonRequest uses default Gson fromJson; missing or JSON-null Java object fields remain null. parseNetworkResponse catches encoding and syntax errors only, not later mapper NPEs.

SiteStore launches fetchSite and fetchSites through coroutineEngine and delegates WP.com REST sites to SiteRestClient.fetchSite()/fetchSites(), confirming the mapper is on the site fetch control-flow path.
Eliminated because the null dereference happens while evaluating the setter argument, before the setter method is entered.
Eliminated as the direct fault because default Gson permissibly maps absent/null fields to null; the crash is triggered by mapper code assuming those fields are non-null.

A WP.com site fetch action reaches SiteRestClient.siteResponseToSiteModel(); when blogging_prompts_settings is non-null but reminders_days is null, line 1137 throws an uncaught NPE and prevents the site fetch from completing.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This is the first unguarded dereference of BloggingPromptsSettings.reminders_days after only the parent blogging_prompts_settings object is null-checked. Since reminders_days is an unannotated Java Map field populated by Gson, it can be null when the key is absent or JSON null; invoking get through the Kotlin indexing operator throws a NullPointerException before the Elvis fallback can run.

This is the sibling unguarded dereference for the null reminders_time variant. reminders_time is an unannotated Java String field, so it may be null after Gson deserialization; split() on null throws NullPointerException, and the catch block only handles NumberFormatException.

final_answer.causal_chain: SiteStore handles FETCH_SITES or FETCH_SITE by calling SiteRestClient.fetchSites()/fetchSite(); those methods request SITE_FIELDS including options and parse the WP.com response into SiteWPComRestResponse using default Gson; Gson can leave BloggingPromptsSettings.reminders_days null because it is an unannotated Java field; siteResponseToSiteModel() checks only that blogging_prompts_settings itself is non-null; line 1137 evaluates it.reminders_days["monday"], which calls Map.get on a null receiver; the resulting NullPointerException is not caught in this block, so it escapes siteResponseToSiteModel() and fails the site fetch.
final_answer.fix_hint: Use null-safe access for the nested fields, e.g. it.reminders_days?.get("monday") ?: false for each day and null-check reminders_time before splitting.
